pattern: Treat each maximal block of one repeated character independently, then binary-search the smallest run length every block can be shrunk to within a shared flip budget.
hints: A flip breaks whatever block it lands in into two shorter pieces of the same character, so separate blocks never need to coordinate with each other over the flip budget.
For a fixed target run length L, work out exactly how many flips one block of length m needs: place a flip every L+1 characters, giving floor(m / (L+1)) flips for that block alone.

Summing that per-block cost over every block gives the total flips needed for a candidate L, and that total only shrinks as L grows, so binary search on L against numOps finds the smallest feasible run length directly.

edge_inputs.description: statement example: a single flip splits one long run of zeros
statement example: two flips fully alternate a short run
statement example: already alternating, no flips available
single character with a spare flip budget that can't help further
several mixed-length runs with a modest shared flip budget
one long uniform run with no flips allowed at all
one long uniform run with more flips available than are ever useful

stress.description: random binary strings up to the full length with a random flip budget
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('01') for _ in range(n))
    num_ops = rng.randint(0, n)
    return [s, num_ops]
